verses.text: A thief must restore double, or fivefold for an ox; a housebreaker slain by night brings no bloodguilt. Restitution. Theft is repaid many times over.
A thief must restore double, or fivefold for an ox; a housebreaker slain by night brings no bloodguilt. Restitution. Theft is repaid many times over.
A thief must restore double, or fivefold for an ox; a housebreaker slain by night brings no bloodguilt. Restitution. Theft is repaid many times over.
A thief must restore double, or fivefold for an ox; a housebreaker slain by night brings no bloodguilt. Restitution. Theft is repaid many times over.
He that lets his beast or fire damage a field must make full restitution. Liable. Damage from carelessness is repaid.
He that lets his beast or fire damage a field must make full restitution. Liable. Damage from carelessness is repaid.
Disputes over goods entrusted are brought before the judges; the guilty pays double. Trust. God provides for honest dealing.
Disputes over goods entrusted are brought before the judges; the guilty pays double. Trust. God provides for honest dealing.
Disputes over goods entrusted are brought before the judges; the guilty pays double. Trust. God provides for honest dealing.
A keeper of another's beast swears or restores, according to the case. Stewardship. We answer for what we hold.
A keeper of another's beast swears or restores, according to the case. Stewardship. We answer for what we hold.
A keeper of another's beast swears or restores, according to the case. Stewardship. We answer for what we hold.
A keeper of another's beast swears or restores, according to the case. Stewardship. We answer for what we hold.
A borrowed beast that dies is made good, unless its owner was with it. Borrowing. What we borrow we must answer for.
A borrowed beast that dies is made good, unless its owner was with it. Borrowing. What we borrow we must answer for.
He that seduces a maid must endow her to be his wife. Responsibility. Sin against another is not without cost.
He that seduces a maid must endow her to be his wife. Responsibility. Sin against another is not without cost.
Witches, and those who sacrifice to false gods, are put to death. Purged. Egregious evils are not tolerated.
Witches, and those who sacrifice to false gods, are put to death. Purged. Egregious evils are not tolerated.
Witches, and those who sacrifice to false gods, are put to death. Purged. Egregious evils are not tolerated.
Vex not the stranger, widow, or fatherless; God will hear their cry. Compassion. God avenges the defenceless.
Vex not the stranger, widow, or fatherless; God will hear their cry. Compassion. God avenges the defenceless.
Vex not the stranger, widow, or fatherless; God will hear their cry. Compassion. God avenges the defenceless.
Vex not the stranger, widow, or fatherless; God will hear their cry. Compassion. God avenges the defenceless.
Lend without usury; restore the poor man's pledged garment by night. Mercy. The poor are treated with tenderness.
Lend without usury; restore the poor man's pledged garment by night. Mercy. The poor are treated with tenderness.
Lend without usury; restore the poor man's pledged garment by night. Mercy. The poor are treated with tenderness.
Revile not the rulers; give God thy firstfruits and firstborn; be holy men. Holiness. Reverence God and honour His order.
Revile not the rulers; give God thy firstfruits and firstborn; be holy men. Holiness. Reverence God and honour His order.
